factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating based on record sales, operating income, orders, and backlog.

evidence.text: For the quarter ended March 31, 2019, the Company posted record sales, operating income, orders and backlog, as well as strong operating income margins, net income and diluted earnings per share.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The factor shows strong positive demand trends with record sales and increasing orders, indicating a strong growth trajectory.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with a 5.6% increase from the previous quarter.

evidence.text: As a result, the Companys backlog of unfilled orders at March 31, 2019 was $1,692.5 million, an increase of $90.4 million or 5.6%, compared with $1,602.1 million at December 31, 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: A 5.6% increase in backlog orders suggests sustained demand and growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, contributing to the overall increase in net sales.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales for the first quarter of 2019 was due to 5% organic sales growth and a 7% increase from acquisitions, partially offset by an unfavorable 2% effect of foreign currency translation.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: 5% organic growth contributes to revenue increase, showing strong internal growth drivers.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are stable as a percentage of net sales but face some pressure from lower margins on recent acquisitions.

evidence.text: Segment operating income, as a percentage of net sales, was 23.4% for the first quarter of 2019 and 2018. EIGs operating margins were 25.2% of net sales for the first quarter of 2019, compared with 25.6% of net sales for the first quarter of 2018. EMGs operating margins were 20.6% of net sales for the first quarter of 2019, compared with 19.9% of net sales for the first quarter of 2018.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Margins are stable but face some pressure from acquisitions, indicating mixed signals.

factors.summary: The company is implementing Operational Excellence initiatives, which are contributing to margin improvements.

evidence.text: The increase in EIGs operating income for the first quarter of 2019 was primarily due to the increase in net sales noted above. EIGs operating margins were 25.2% of net sales for the first quarter of 2019, compared with 25.6% of net sales for the first quarter of 2018. EIGs first quarter of 2019 operating margins declined primarily from the impact of lower margins on recent acquisitions, which had a 1.5% negative impact, partially offset by the benefits of the Groups Operational Excellence initiatives. EMGs operating margins were 20.6% of net sales for the first quarter of 2019, compared with 19.9% of net sales for the first quarter of 2018. The increase in EMGs operating income and operating margins for the first quarter of 2019 were primarily due to the increase in net sales noted above, as well as the benefits of the Groups Operational Excellence initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Implementation of Operational Excellence initiatives is improving margins, reflecting effective cost management.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with a 6.5% increase compared to the previous quarter.

evidence.text: Free cash flow (cash flow provided by operating activities less capital expenditures) was $174.8 million for the first three months of 2019, compared with $165.1 million for the first three months of 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: A 6.5% increase in free cash flow shows improving financial health and cash generation.

factors.summary: The company has successfully integrated recent acquisitions, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: The Company achieved these results from organic sales growth in both EIG and EMG, contributions from the 2018 acquisitions of Spectro Scientific Corporation in November 2018, Forza Silicon Corporation (Forza) and Telular Corporation in October 2018, Motec in June 2018 and SoundCom in April 2018, as well as our Operational Excellence initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful integration of recent acquisitions is contributing to revenue growth, indicating effective strategic moves.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a tailwind, with strong demand and positive market conditions supporting the company's results.

evidence.text: For the quarter ended March 31, 2019, the Company posted record sales, operating income, orders and backlog, as well as strong operating income margins, net income and diluted earnings per share.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong economic conditions with high demand are acting as a tailwind, supporting company performance.

factors.summary: Management expects continued positive impact from the full year 2018 acquisitions and Operational Excellence initiatives.

evidence.text: For 2019, the Companys record backlog, the full year impact of the 2018 acquisitions and continued focus on and implementation of Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on the remainder of the Companys 2019 results.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its share repurchase program, signaling confidence.

evidence.text: Additional financing activities for the first three months of 2019 included cash dividends paid of $31.8 million, compared with $32.3 million for the first three months of 2018. Effective February 12, 2019, the Companys Board of Directors approved an increase of $500 million in the authorization for the repurchase of the Companys common stock.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging, with a reduction in net debt and improved debt-to-capital ratios.

evidence.text: At March 31, 2019, total debt, net was $2,472.4 million, compared with $2,632.7 million at December 31, 2018. The debt-to-capital ratio was 35.7% at March 31, 2019, compared with 38.3% at December 31, 2018. The net debt-to-capital ratio (total debt, net less cash and cash equivalents divided by the sum of net debt and stockholders equity) was 32.1% at March 31, 2019, compared with 34.9% at December 31, 2018.
